key_insight: The paper replaces repeated online stochastic optimization solves with a learned proxy that predicts near-optimal feasible decisions in a single forward pass.
engineering_relevance: This enables sub-second fulfillment decisions for real-time operations like omnichannel order assignment, where solver latency is too slow for peak-demand planning.
method: Train a scenario-embedded neural network offline on labels from contextual sample average approximation (C-SAA).
Use a decoder at inference time to enforce feasibility of the predicted fulfillment plan.
Optimize with a composite loss combining label imitation, constraint-violation penalties, and self-supervised cost alignment.
evidence_signal: In a simulator calibrated from JD.com transactional data, the proxy was about 2800x faster than online finite-sample C-SAA, cut realized fulfillment cost by 3.3% versus that reference, and reduced total cost by at least 10.7% versus established policies.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and representativeness of solver-generated training labels and simulator calibration, so distribution shift could weaken gains in deployment.
paper_title: Learning Optimization Proxies for Sequential Contextual Stochastic Programs: An Order Fulfillment Application
